subject: Project Management
title: Kanban vs Scrum
description: A quick, practical guide to Kanban vs Scrum.

hook.text: Scrum prescribes sprints, roles, and events. Kanban prescribes almost nothing.

buildup.text: Scrum changes everything at once. Kanban says: start where you are, improve incrementally.

discovery.text: Scrum works best with dedicated teams. Kanban works even with shared resources and interruptions.

twist.text: Many teams blend both — 'Scrumban' — using sprints for planning but Kanban boards for flow.

climax.text: It's not Kanban or Scrum. It's which tool fits which situation. Smart teams use both.

punchline.text: The best method is the one that fits your team.

quiz.question: How does Kanban differ from Scrum in terms of adoption?
options: Kanban starts where you are; Scrum requires full transformation
Kanban requires more roles than Scrum
